target.name: Release hardening cell
target.current_state: Release review, source verification, and red-team findings are handled by separate ad hoc tasks.
target.proposed_state: A standing release hardening cell integrates S2, Protection, Red Team, S6, and Recorder outputs for release-required missions.
essential_functions: Compare release target to reviewed target.
Trace final claims to source-map entries.
Block EEFI or restricted context from final output.
Queue commander decisions for high residual risk or release exceptions.

capability_gap.problem: Release-required missions repeatedly need the same cross-functional controls, but current ad hoc coordination risks target mismatch and unsupported claims.
evidence: release-integration fixtures cover missing or invalid release review.
source-map linter now guards official source coverage.
SOF TF and department collaboration charters both require independent release/protection review.
operational_impact: External release can be delayed by missing source trace.
Internal handoff review can be confused with final output release if controls are not integrated.
Critical release finding may miss commander queue if protection and red team are not synchronized.
capability_gap.why_now: Release controls have grown from policy into multiple runner contracts and need a stable cell-level ownership model.

alternatives_considered.reason_not_sufficient: SOP text does not assign cross-functional output ownership or fixture coverage.

alternatives_considered.option: Train S3 executor to perform release review
alternatives_considered.reason_not_sufficient: Executor self-review violates independent protection and release review separation.

alternatives_considered.option: Run SOF TF for every release
alternatives_considered.reason_not_sufficient: SOF TF is for high-risk exceptions; routine release-required work needs a standing cell with lower activation overhead.

doctrine: Update force-structure-change-policy with release cell example.
Keep release review policy separate from execution approval policy.
organization: Create release hardening cell as a unit-level coordination node.
Use supported/supporting relationships from department collaboration charter.
training: Require release target mismatch fixture and EEFI release-block fixture for cell readiness.
leadership: Commander retains external release approval and high risk acceptance.
facilities_context: Use source-map, release review, event log, and dashboard projection as shared context.
policy: No release without target-matched release review.
No high residual risk acceptance outside Commander.

commander_retained_decisions: High or critical residual risk acceptance.
prohibited_changes: Using internal handoff review to authorize final output release.
Expanding release target without new review.
approval_evidence: Decision packet approving release hardening cell creation.
Passing release integration and source-map coverage fixtures.

resources.context_budget: Cell receives release candidate, release target, source-map delta, red-team findings, and releasability metadata only.
resources.time_budget: Run targeted release fixtures and source-map linter before release go/no-go.
dashboard_or_runner_cost: Add cell status only if release-required workload remains recurring.
fallback: If release runner fails, block release and route to Protection.
If source-map linter fails, route to S2 and Recorder.
If Red Team critical finding remains, route to Commander decision packet.

metl_tasks: Route high residual risk to Commander.
backup_or_successor: Protection lead backup: RED_TEAM for block recommendation only.
Recorder backup: S6 for event log preservation.
Commander retained authority does not transfer.

activation_steps: Run release, source-map, and full validator fixtures.
data_migration: Existing release review docs remain source of truth.
Future release-required missions reference the cell.
deactivation_or_sunset: If release-required workload drops below recurring threshold for two review cycles, disband or reduce cell to SOP.
If automation replaces manual integration, reduce cell to protection reviewer plus runner owner.
rollback: Return release work to department collaboration charter.
Preserve schemas, fixtures, and AAR evidence unless obsolete.

mop: Cell outputs are represented in handoff and AAR.
moe: Release-required missions no longer confuse internal handoff and final output review.
Unsupported claims are blocked or marked as assumptions before final release.
Commander queue receives high residual risk and release exceptions.
aar_trigger: Any unsupported claim reaches release candidate.
sunset_condition: No release-required workload for two review cycles.
Automation covers source trace, release review, and commander queue without manual cell integration.
